description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs:151-164: the local `s` (assigned at line 155, used at line 164 in `entry.set_password(&s)`) is the decoded password string. Single-letter names are appropriate for short scopes where the type is the only meaningful information (e.g. `for c in chars`). Here the lifetime spans 14 lines across an async closure, and the variable is the secret payload — its semantic is 'password to set', not 'string'.

Suggested rewrite: rename to `password` (matches the keyring crate's API: `entry.set_password(&password)`). Pure readability win, no behavior change.

This is taste-adjacent but the keyring crate's own naming uses `password` for this binding, so consistency with the immediate dep argues for it.
title: Single-letter local "s" for decoded password — name for meaning, not type
